versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 6 For this is what the LORD says concerning the house of the king of Judah:
but I will surely turn you into a desert,
7 I will appoint destroyers against you,
and they will cut down the choicest of your cedars
and throw them into the fire.
8 And many nations will pass by this city and ask one another, ‘Why has the LORD done such a thing to this great city?’
9 Then people will reply, ‘Because they have forsaken the covenant of the LORD their God and have worshiped and served other gods.’ ”

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 22**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **A Warning about the Palace** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 22** remains the first control on meaning.
